name: Claude Runtime Promotion Policy
goal: Prevent fragile scripts, handwritten config, or ambiguous optional capabilities from entering Claude's live runtime plane without explicit governance.

promotionChecklist.statement: The asset has one clear runtime purpose and a named control-plane owner.
All Claude-home path construction resolves through scripts/runtime_paths.py or an explicitly approved portable entrypoint helper.
If the asset affects settings.json or mcp.json, it integrates through the canonical generator rather than direct hand editing.
The asset is represented in generated runtime truth where operators would reasonably expect to discover it.
Required live runtime flows do not depend on shell assumptions that the target platform cannot guarantee.
Optional integrations declare required environment activation conditions instead of remaining enabled-but-broken.
The asset does not reference sibling-runtime, backup, or historical roots except through explicit bridge policy.
The asset is visible to doctor, invariants, or both when its failure would matter operationally.
The asset has targeted tests or a deterministic smoke path that can be rerun after changes.
Human-readable runtime docs are updated in the same architectural batch.

blockers: hardcoded Claude-home or sibling-runtime absolute paths
bash-only dependency in required Windows runtime flow

requirements: must point to one canonical owner
must be documented as compatibility only
must not become the source of truth
remove live references before archival move
preserve historical material only behind archive boundaries
